name: Syrian or Lebanese Bread
recipeIngredient: 2 packages Active dry yeast or 1 yeast cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Sprinkle the yeast over water and stir in 1 tablespoon of flour and sugar.
Set aside to rise for about 10 minutes.
2. Sift together the 3 cups of flour and salt.
3. Add the yeast mixture to the sifted flour mixture.
4. Knead, adding one cup of water gradually until a stiff but not dry dough forms.
5. Place the dough in a warm place to rise until doubled in bulk.
6. Punch down the dough. Divide into 6 to 8 round balls.
7. Place each ball on a lightly floured surface and roll out with a rolling pin
to form circles about 1/4 inch thick.
8. Cover the rolls with a kitchen towel and let them rise again for 15-25 minutes.
9. Brush the rolls with milk.
10. Preheat the oven to 500°F (260°C).
11. Place the bread on baking sheets and bake for 8 minutes until puffed up.
12. Turn each roll over so that both sides brown evenly.
13. Allow the bread to cool slightly before serving. It is excellent for sandwiches.
